site.name: RCOS - Regenerative Community Operating System
site.description: RCOS is an open-source, shared system for organizing intentional communities. Clear, ready-to-use structures for decision-making, roles, resource sharing, and handling conflicts.
language_switcher.fallback_tooltip: Not yet translated — will show English on this page
home.title: RCOS - Regenerative Community Operating System
home.intro: RCOS is an open-source, shared system for organizing intentional communities. It gives you clear, ready-to-use structures for decision-making, roles, resource sharing, and handling conflicts — so you can focus on building your community instead of figuring everything out from scratch. Explore the core system, optional modules, and how to apply them.

home.topics.subheading: Main areas of regenerative design and implementation

footer.tagline: A modular operating system that defines how intentional communities organize — from governance and roles to resource sharing and conflict resolution — in support of resilience, fairness, and regeneration.
